title: Technical Support Engineer (bilingual English/French)
description: Who We AreVerkada is transforming how organizations protect their people and places with an integrated, AI-powered platform. A leader in cloud physical security, Verkada helps organizations strengthen safety and efficiency through one connected software platform that includes solutions for video security, access control, air quality sensors, alarms, intercoms, and visitor management.Over 30,000 organizations worldwide, including more than 100 companies in the Fortune 500, trust Verkada as their physical security layer for easier management, intelligent control, and scalable deployments. Founded in 2016, Verkada has expanded rapidly with 15 offices and 2,200+ full-time employees.About The RoleVerkada is expanding rapidly in the EMEA region. We are looking for Technical Support Engineers to support our growing base of enterprise customers in our London office. As a member of Technical Support Engineering, you will join a skilled team supporting the entire line of Verkada cloud-managed building security products. Working independently and collaboratively, you will solve technical issues and advise customers on our platform. Our team is the bridge between customers, Engineering, and Product Management. This role will report into one of our Technical Support Engineering Managers and is based on-site 5 days per week in our London office.What You'll DoFull-stack troubleshooting of the entire Verkada platform including, network, hardware, software, and electrical issuesProvide best practice design, installation, and configuration expertise to enterprise customersCommunicate technical matters to customers both orally and in writingOwn customer issues from creation to resolutionCollaborate with Engineering and Product teams to test new products and identify bugsWrite technical knowledge base articles and whitepapersStaying up to date on the latest products and industry technologiesBuild tools and processes that will scaleWhat You BringBilingual in French and English, both verbally and in writingBachelor's or Master's degree in related technical discipline: Networking, Telecommunications, Systems Administration, Information Technology, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or Cybersecurity1+ years professional or internship experience in a technical support engineering or IT roleStrong understanding of network fundamentals: HTTP/HTTPS, TLS, VLANs, TCP/IP, DNS, NAT, DHCP, 802.11, firewalls, Ethernet/Cabling, PoE, and software systemsExperience working with Linux, APIs, Python, or other coding languages is a plusAbility to communicate complex technical matters both orally and in writingExcellent customer service skills and ability to multitask and prioritize in a fast-paced environmentDemonstrated problem-solving skills and attention to detailIndustry certifications are a plus: CCNP, CCNA, CCENT, CCDA, CWNP, CWNA, MCSE, CEH, Network+, Security+, A+Employee BenefitsVerkada is committed to fostering a workplace environment that prioritizes the holistic health and wellbeing of our employees and their families by offering comprehensive wellness perks, benefits, and resources. Our benefits and perks programs include, but are not limited to:Healthcare programs that can be tailored to meet the personal health and financial well-being needs. Premiums are 100% covered for the employee under most plans and 100% for family premiumsPrivate medical and dental coveragePaid parental leave policy & fertility benefitsRetirement saving optionsTime off to relax and recharge through our paid holidays, firmwide extended holidays, flexible PTO and personal sick timeWellness/fitness benefitsProfessional development stipendHealthy lunches provided dailyCommuter benefitsAdditional InformationWe do sponsor and take over sponsorship of employment visas for this role. If we make you an offer, we will make every reasonable effort to get you a visa. Verkada Is An Equal Opportunity EmployerAs an equal opportunity employer, Verkada is committed to providing employment opportunities to all individuals. All applicants for positions at Verkada will be treated without regard to race, color, ethnicity, religion, sex, gender, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, age, marital status, veteran status, pregnancy, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.Your application will be handled in accordance with our Candidate Privacy Policy.

title: IT Support Field Engineer Within South London
description: Full Job DescriptionIT Support Field Engineer – South London Home based South London | Hybrid workingPermanent | 37.5 hours per week (Mon–Fri, 8am–4pm)Salary: Up to £35,000 per annum (dependent on experience and qualifications) + £3,000 car allowance. In 2025, Outcomes First Group were officially certified as a ‘Great Place to Work’ for the sixth year running. We’re proud to be part of the 4-Day Working Week trial, giving our teams more time to recharge while still earning 100% of their pay.*Outcomes First Group is the largest independent provider of high-quality special needs education and care in the UK. We deliver research-led, personalised education that helps every child thrive.Our vision is to empower every child with a world-class education that nurtures potential and inspires lifelong learning. Our mission is to unlock that potential through personalised learning and opportunity, and our promise is simple: we listen, work together, and hold each other accountable to make the remarkable happen.*Please note: 4DWW is subject to successful completion of your probation period and is not a contractual benefit.About UsAt Outcomes First Group, we believe every child can thrive when given the right environment for success. As one of the world’s leading providers in our sector, we deliver exceptional, research-led education tailored to each individual, with smaller class sizes and personalised learning plans.Our vision is to empower every child with a world-class education that nurtures potential and inspires lifelong learning. Our mission is to unlock that potential through personalised learning and opportunity. And our promise is simple: we listen, we work together, and we are accountable to one another to make the remarkable happen.The Role: We are seeking an experienced IT Support Field Engineer to provide 2nd/3rd line support across multiple sites in the South London. You will assist with incidents, service requests, and problem resolution, escalating where necessary, and support the wider IT team with technology rollouts and investigations.What you'll do: Troubleshoot and resolve incidents, service requests, and major problems.Serve as first point of escalation for 1st/2nd line engineers and specific sites.Manage site relationships and support infrastructure, applications, devices, and networking equipment (Cisco Meraki, Unifi).Administer user accounts, permissions, and mobile device configurations (Active Directory, Office 365, Azure AD, Google Workspace, Jamf, Intune).Conduct root cause analysis, contribute to knowledge sharing, and support cyber and P1 infrastructure incidents.Install applications, configure hardware (laptops, mobiles, tablets), support printers, cloud telephony, servers, and web filtering.What we're looking for: 3–5 years’ experience in 2nd/3rd line IT support, preferably within ITIL frameworks or MSPs.Strong technical knowledge of desktop/server platforms, networking, virtualization, and cloud solutions.Excellent troubleshooting, communication, and organisational skills.Desirable: IT qualification, VOIP/telephony knowledge, and network management experience.Other Requirements:Travel to schools and office locations as required.Commitment to safeguarding children and vulnerable adults.Maintain professional development and training.BenefitsLife AssurancePension scheme with contribution options“Your Wellbeing Matters” mental and physical health supportFamily Growth Support, including enhanced maternity and paternity leaveFlexible benefits via Vista (discounts, Cycle to Work, Electric Car Scheme, critical illness cover and more)We are committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and young people. All applicants will be subject to social media checks and successful applicants to a fully enhanced DBS.We are an Equal Opportunities Employer.UK applicants only. This role does not offer sponsorship.We reserve the right to close the vacancy early if we receive a high volume of suitable applications.Outcomes First Group is committed to carrying out a fair, thorough and efficient recruitment process in line with Keeping Children Safe in Education. Whilst we aim to keep applicants informed throughout, Outcomes First Group does not accept liability for any loss of earnings or other associated costs incurred by applicants as a result of delays or changes in the compliance process. All stages of the compliance process are subject to necessary safeguarding checks and statutory requirements, which may affect timescales.

The successful applicant will be sent out to client sites from the off and expected to hold their own in some of the leading financial institutions in the space. They will be given the opportunity to run their own projects and develop their skills among some of the leading professionals in the financial services. For immediate inquiries please contact Harriet Lamplugh de Smith at Harrington Starr on 07906 861860 / harriet@harringtonstarr.com

title: IT Support Field Engineer – South London
description: IT Support Field Engineer – South LondonHome based South London| Hybrid workingPermanent | 37.5 hours per week (Mon–Fri, 8am–4pm)Salary: Up to £35,000 per annum (dependent on experience and qualifications) + £3,000 car allowance. In 2025, Outcomes First Group were officially certified as a ‘Great Place to Work’ for the sixth year running. We’re proud to be part of the 4-Day Working Week trial, giving our teams more time to recharge while still earning 100% of their pay.*Outcomes First Group is the largest independent provider of high-quality special needs education and care in the UK. We deliver research-led, personalised education that helps every child thrive.Our vision is to empower every child with a world-class education that nurtures potential and inspires lifelong learning. Our mission is to unlock that potential through personalised learning and opportunity, and our promise is simple: we listen, work together, and hold each other accountable to make the remarkable happen.*Please note: 4DWW is subject to successful completion of your probation period and is not a contractual benefit.About UsAt Outcomes First Group, we believe every child can thrive when given the right environment for success. Our mission is to unlock that potential through personalised learning and opportunity. And our promise is simple: we listen, we work together, and we are accountable to one another to make the remarkable happen.The Role: We are seeking an experienced IT Support Field Engineer to provide 2nd/3rd line support across multiple sites in the South London. You will assist with incidents, service requests, and problem resolution, escalating where necessary, and support the wider IT team with technology rollouts and investigations.What you'll do: Troubleshoot and resolve incidents, service requests, and major problems.Serve as first point of escalation for 1st/2nd line engineers and specific sites.Manage site relationships and support infrastructure, applications, devices, and networking equipment (Cisco Meraki, Unifi).Administer user accounts, permissions, and mobile device configurations (Active Directory, Office 365, Azure AD, Google Workspace, Jamf, Intune).Conduct root cause analysis, contribute to knowledge sharing, and support cyber and P1 infrastructure incidents.Install applications, configure hardware (laptops, mobiles, tablets), support printers, cloud telephony, servers, and web filtering.What we're looking for: 3–5 years’ experience in 2nd/3rd line IT support, preferably within ITIL frameworks or MSPs.Strong technical knowledge of desktop/server platforms, networking, virtualization, and cloud solutions.Excellent troubleshooting, communication, and organisational skills.Desirable: IT qualification, VOIP/telephony knowledge, and network management experience.Other Requirements:Travel to schools and office locations as required.Commitment to safeguarding children and vulnerable adults.Maintain professional development and training.BenefitsLife AssurancePension scheme with contribution options“Your Wellbeing Matters” mental and physical health supportFamily Growth Support, including enhanced maternity and paternity leaveFlexible benefits via Vista (discounts, Cycle to Work, Electric Car Scheme, critical illness cover and more)We are committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and young people.All applicants will be subject to social media checks and successful applicants to a fully enhanced DBS.We are an Equal Opportunities Employer.UK applicants only. This role does not offer sponsorship.We reserve the right to close the vacancy early if we receive a high volume of suitable applications.Outcomes First Group is committed to carrying out a fair, thorough and efficient recruitment process in line with Keeping Children Safe in Education. Whilst we aim to keep applicants informed throughout, Outcomes First Group does not accept liability for any loss of earnings or other associated costs incurred by applicants as a result of delays or changes in the compliance process.All stages of the compliance process are subject to necessary safeguarding checks and statutory requirements, which may affect timescales.

title: Techbar & AV Engineer, EUC Onsite Support
description: ABOUT US:LSEG (London Stock Exchange Group) is more than a diversified global financial markets infrastructure and data business. We are dedicated, open-access partners with a dedication to excellence in delivering the services our customers expect from us. With extensive experience, deep knowledge and worldwide presence across financial markets, we enable businesses and economies around the world to fund innovation, manage risk and create jobs. It’s how we’ve contributed to supporting the financial stability and growth of communities and economies globally for more than 300 years. Through a comprehensive suite of trusted financial market infrastructure services – and our open-access model – we provide the flexibility, stability and trust that enable our customers to pursue their ambitions with confidence and clarity.LSEG is headquartered in the United Kingdom, with significant operations in 70 countries across EMEA, North America, Latin America and Asia Pacific. We employ 25,000 people globally, more than half located in Asia Pacific. LSEG’s ticker symbol is LSEG.OUR PEOPLE:People are at the heart of what we do and drive the success of our business. Our culture of connecting, creating opportunity and delivering excellence shape how we think, how we do things and how we help our people fulfil their potential. We embrace diversity and actively seek to attract individuals with unique backgrounds and perspectives. We break down barriers and encourage teamwork, enabling innovation and rapid development of solutions that make a difference. Our workplace generates an enriching and rewarding experience for our people and customers alike. Our vision is to build an inclusive culture in which everyone feels encouraged to fulfil their potential.We know that real personal growth cannot be achieved by simply climbing a career ladder – which is why we encourage and enable a wealth of avenues and interesting opportunities for everyone to broaden and deepen their skills and expertise. As a global organisation spanning 70 countries and one rooted in a culture of growth, opportunity, diversity and innovation, LSEG is a place where everyone can grow, develop and fulfil your potential with meaningful careers!!Role PurposeThe Junior Engineer supports the setup, operation, maintenance, and troubleshooting of audio-visual and video conferencing technologies across meeting rooms, event spaces, and corporate environments. This role is hands-on and customer-facing, ensuring reliable AV services and an excellent user experience. You will also be required to assist with onsite support issues at desk, IE monitor swap out, keyboard and mouse issues.Key ResponsibilitiesAV Support & OperationsAssist in the setup, testing, and operation of AV equipment for meetings, presentations, and events.Provide first-line support for issues related to projectors, displays, microphones, speakers, control systems, and video conferencing platforms.Support day-to-day service desk requests related to AV needs.Help with room checks and preventive maintenance of AV facilities.Technical TroubleshootingDiagnose and resolve common AV issues (e.g., connectivity problems, audio dropouts, display failures).Escalate complex technical problems to senior engineers or vendors when necessary.Installation & MaintenanceAssist with installation and configuration of new AV hardware under supervision.Perform regular checks to ensure equipment is functioning correctly.Help maintain inventory of AV equipment and accessories.Collaboration & Customer ServiceDeliver technical support with a helpful, professional, and customer‑focused attitude.Work closely with IT teams, facility management, and external suppliers/vendors.Support live events with basic AV operation (e.g., microphones, switchers, cameras).DocumentationMaintain accurate documentation of equipment, room configurations, and troubleshooting steps.Update user guides and simple how‑to instructions for end users when required.Technical SkillsRequired Skills & ExperienceBasic understanding of AV technologies:Displays, projectors, DSPs, microphones, speakersVideoconferencing systems (Teams Rooms, Zoom Rooms, etc.)Basic networking concepts (IP addresses, cabling, bandwidth)Familiarity with AV control systems (Cisco, Polycom, Crestron, AMX, Extron) is beneficial but not required.Ability to use common troubleshooting tools (cable testers, network utilities).Professional SkillsStrong communication and interpersonal abilities.Good organisational and time‑management skills.Willingness to learn new technologies and undergo training.Problem-solving mindset with attention to detail.QualificationsA-levels, vocational training, or equivalent technical qualification (preferred but not essential).Any AV-related certifications (e.g., AVIXA CTS) are an advantage.Basic IT certifications (CompTIA A+, N+, or equivalent) beneficial.ExperienceExperience in an AV, IT support, or related technical role.Experience supporting meetings or events in a professional environment is desirable.Personal AttributesCustomer-focused approach.Proactive and keen to learn.Comfortable working in fast-paced or corporate environments.Team player with a positive attitude.Career ProgressionWith demonstrated growth and technical capability, this role can progress to:AV Engineer / Mid-Level AV TechnicianSenior AV EngineerAV Systems SpecialistEvents TechnicianIT/AV Hybrid Support EngineerOnsite Support EngineerCareer Stage:AssociateLondon Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) Information:Join us and be part of a team that values innovation, quality, and continuous improvement. If you're ready to take your career to the next level and make a significant impact, we'd love to hear from you.LSEG is a leading global financial markets infrastructure and data provider. Our purpose is driving financial stability, empowering economies and enabling customers to create sustainable growth.Our purpose is the foundation on which our culture is built. Our values of Integrity, Partnership, Excellence and Change underpin our purpose and set the standard for everything we do, every day. They go to the heart of who we are and guide our decision making and everyday actions.Working with us means that you will be part of a dynamic organisation of 25,000 people across 65 countries. However, we will value your individuality and enable you to bring your true self to work so you can help enrich our diverse workforce.We are proud to be an equal opportunities employer. This means that we do not discriminate on the basis of anyone’s race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. Conforming with applicable law, we can reasonably accommodate applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs.You will be part of a collaborative and creative culture where we encourage new ideas. We are committed to sustainability across our global business and we are proud to partner with our customers to help them meet their sustainability objectives. Our charity, the LSEG Foundation provides charitable grants to community groups that help people access economic opportunities and build a secure future with financial independence. Colleagues can get involved through fundraising and volunteering.LSEG offers a range of tailored benefits and support, including healthcare, retirement planning, paid volunteering days and wellbeing initiatives.Please take a moment to read this privacy notice carefully, as it describes what personal information London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) (we) may hold about you, what it’s used for, and how it’s obtained, your rights and how to contact us as a data subject.If you are submitting as a Recruitment Agency Partner, it is essential and your responsibility to ensure that candidates applying to LSEG are aware of this privacy notice.

title: Technical Engineer Placement Year
description: The RoleRoleWe are currently looking for bright students studying a computer related degree to join our close-knit technical team. This diverse role will give you a hands-on taster into several different areas of the tech industry. Ideally the candidate would have a keen interest in two or more of the below areas:SQL Server Database Administration Cyber Security & Networks Computer Hardware – Personal and Enterprise Windows & Server Administration You will be encouraged to continuously participate in the designing, maintaining and upgrading of services that are critical to the NHS and many healthcare organisations around the world.If you enjoy solving technical problems, have an interest in technology and thrive in a changeable environment then this role is for you.About TPPTPP is a world-leader in Digital Health. From apps enabling patients to manage their own care to comprehensive hospital solutions, TPP delivers technology that connects everyone involved in healthcare delivery. For over twenty years, we have been applying the most advanced technology to provide a safe and secure environment to deliver care. Our aim is to improve access to healthcare data, making sure it is available whenever and wherever it is needed. We want to help people make the best use of that data, to empower clinicians and citizens, and to create the healthcare systems of the future.‘TPP has been consistently recognised as an outstanding graduate employer. Since 2017, we have consistently featured in The Job Crowds’ "Top Company for Graduates to Work For”, and were the overall winners for 2024 in addition to awards for 'Best Salary', 'Benefits' and 'Responsibility’. We have also been consistently named in the Times Top 100 Graduate Employers list’Salary and BenefitsYou will get a salary of £30,000 and will receive a range of benefits including:Fantastic holiday entitlement Regular social events Opportunities to travel internationally £300 birthday meal allowance Pub Fridays RequirementsCurrently studying a computer related degree Minimum ABB/AAC at A-level including either Maths or IT Successful candidates will usually be contacted within two weeks. Please be aware that applications will be kept on file for up to 12 months.

title: Network Support Engineer
description: About AspireAt Aspire Technology Solutions, we look for dynamic individuals who want to work for one of the fastest growing IT and Cyber Security providers in the UK. It is an exciting time to join us on our journey as we grow and expand! Our mission: To deliver technology like no other!About The RoleWe are looking for an Network Support Engineer with a strong technical background and passion for problem solving.Working alongside the wider Service Desk, you will work towards continually driving home technical solutions whilst putting our customers needs first. This could involve fixing a technical fault, answering a query, fulfilling a service request anything that is needed to allow the user to return to work.Our ideal candidate would be an experienced engineer, who is an instinctive problem solver and able to drive technical solutions. Strong communication skills will help you to diagnose the customers issue and explain technical issues in a way that non-experts can understand. Efficiency is also important to succeed, as you will be managing a diverse workload.Communication with customers will take place over the phone and via email whilst utilising fault-finding programmes that assist you to diagnose and fix customer problems remotely. Customer service is key to success in the role living our values are woven through everything we do to guarantee that our service delivery is second to none.What you will be doing Provide remote network support, diagnosing and resolving incidents and service requests relating to:Network connectivity and performance issuesFirewall management, including rule changes, security policies and VPN configuration across multiple vendors such as FortiGate, Sophos and Check PointNetwork fault diagnosis and resolution, including switching, wireless access points and configuration changesLayer 2 switching issues, including VLAN configuration and spanning tree troubleshooting across vendors such as Cisco, Extreme and UniFiAct as the technical lead for customers during service changes, incident recovery and restoration activities.Build and maintain strong working relationships with teams across Aspire, collaborating effectively to deliver customer outcomes.Manage incidents and service requests in line with agreed SLAs, effectively prioritising workload while handling multiple concurrent issues in accordance with established processes and best practices.Contribute to continual service improvement by identifying opportunities to enhance efficiency, quality and personal professional development.Support and collaborate with colleagues as a key member of the team, assisting with complex issues and required service changes. You will suit the role if you haveExperience working in multi-vendor network environments e.g. Fortinet, Cisco, Palo Alto, Check Point or Aruba technologies.Previous experience in an ISP, MSP or similar network support environment.Exposure to ISP-grade connectivity, including Dedicated Internet Access (DIA), Ethernet services, and Layer 2 / Layer 3 networking.Strong understanding of routing (BGP, OSPF), switching (VLANs, STP) and wireless networking, with the ability to troubleshoot end-to-end solutions.Excellent communication skills, with the ability to clearly explain technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.A customer-focused approach, with strong ownership, analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.Hands-on experience with FortiGate, FortiManager or FortiAnalyzer, as well as associated infrastructure such as switches and wireless access points.Fortinet NSE certifications (NSE 4, 5, 6 or 7) are highly desirable.Equivalent vendor certifications such as CCNA/CCNP, JNCIS or PCNSE would be advantageous. Our valuesWe understand that you might not have all of the qualifications and experiences that we think are key to succeeding in this role therefore we provide continuous learning and development at Aspire which is at the heart of everything we do. As long as you are passionate, eager to learn and driven to be the best at what you do and live our values this role may be a fit for you.Salary £30,000 to £40,000 per annum (depending on experience)BenefitsHere at Aspire we offer a great benefits package and a competitive salary. Being a people-centric organisation is what sets us apart we strive to look after our people in the best way we can!Travel benefits including free parking, subsidized travel passes and much more!Health Cash PlanCycle to Work SchemeNetwork BenefitsEmployee Assistance ProgramEnhanced Annual Leave Entitlement (increasing with length of service)Enhanced Maternity, Paternity and Parental SchemesEnhanced Pension SchemeHome & Tech Purchase SchemeElectric Vehicle Salary Sacrifice SchemeEmployee referral schemeNew business referral schemeDiscounted Gym MembershipLife Assurance and Critical Illness cover Location and Working ArrangementsYou will be based at Aspires Gateshead Head Office, with travel as required.Hours of Work Full time (37.5 hours per week), Monday to FridayEquality, Inclusion and Diversity at Aspire Aspire is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating a culture that respects and values each others differences, that promotes dignity, equality and diversity that encourages individuals to develop their true potential.Interview Process We generally conduct interviews in three stages, lasting approximately 2-3 weeks, depending on everyones calendars.Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a pre-screening call via Teams to discuss your experience and what youre looking for in your next role.Successful candidates will be invited to an interview with the Hiring Manager.If successful in an interview, candidates will be invited to an informal interview with key stakeholders at Aspire to meet the wider team(s).#AspireForMoreLNKD1_UKTJ

title: Senior Cloud Technician
description: What you'll be doing: Join Atech as a Senior Cloud Technician, where you’ll be working closely with law firms and legal professionals, supporting the cloud environments that keep their practices running smoothly.In this role, you’ll handle escalated tickets from 1st line, taking ownership of more complex issues and ensuring our clients receive fast, expert-level support.You’ll support and maintain Windows Terminal Servers, alongside working with Azure-based platforms. A large part of your role will focus on the legal sector’s core applications - especially Partner for Windows (P4W).Your technical expertise combined with excellent customer handling skills will make a real difference to our clients operations. We want to hear from you if you: Can feasibly commute to RomfordHave experience with Microsoft 365 and a good understanding of Azure and IntuneAre comfortable working with Windows Server and Active DirectoryHave previously worked in a customer support or service desk environmentCan manage and prioritise your workload effectively in a fast‑paced settingCommunicate clearly and confidently, both in writing and verbally — especially with demanding customersAre eager to learn new technologies and continually expand your skillset(Desirable) Have experience working within the legal sector as part of an internal IT support team What's in it for me? £30,000 per annum + benefits Who you'll be doing it for: Atech part of the Iomart Group is a highly accredited Microsoft Partner who delivers transformed technology with managed services. Our team of certified Microsoft experts align with your team to deliver an excellent service tailored to your individual needs, 24/7/365.Our services support 25,000 users globally and proactively monitor 45,000+ devices in key areas:Azure infrastructure managed serviceModern Workplace: Office 365, Microsoft 365, and Azure Virtual DesktopManaged Security and SOC with Microsoft Defender, Sentinel  What to do next: Please click apply if you like the sound of this role. If you do not have an up to date CV or want to have a chat about the role first, please contact us on careers@iomart.com.We’re an equal opportunities employer and want our vacancies to be available to all, so if you need us to make any reasonable adjustments during the process then just let us know.

title: IT Infrastructure Technician
description: If you're a hands-on IT Support Analyst or 2nd Line Technician with solid Microsoft 365 admin experience and you want more ownership, this role gives you exactly that.You'll be the day-to-day IT go-to for a busy on-site workforce, supporting colleagues across office and operational teams. You will not be doing this alone, though. You'll have support from an experienced Business Systems team, plus an external MSP for escalations. Day-to-day, you'll be the person keeping things moving, solving problems, and making sure people can get on with their work.This role suits someone with around 3 to 4 years' IT support experience who is confident working independently, enjoys helping people, and wants to keep building their Microsoft 365, Entra ID and Intune exposure.What You'll Need (the Essentials)You do not need to already hold the title IT Infrastructure Technician. If you have been an IT Support Analyst, Service Desk Engineer, IT Support Engineer or 2nd Line Technician, and you can run with tickets and admin tasks without hand-holding, you should feel confident applying.Key RequirementsAround 3 to 4 years' experience in IT support, helpdesk or service desk roles.Strong working knowledge of Microsoft 365 Admin Centre.Exposure to Entra ID (identity/access, users, groups).Commercial exposure to Intune.Working knowledge of on-prem Active Directory (users/devices, day-to-day admin).Experience setting up, re-imaging and enrolling laptops and devices.Confident troubleshooting across hardware, software, printers and peripherals.Comfortable prioritising tickets and owning issues through to resolution.Clear communication and a genuinely helpful, proactive approach.Good English verbal and written communication skills.The right to work in the UK without sponsorship.What You'll Be DoingAs the IT Infrastructure Technician, you'll provide practical, day-to-day IT support and keep users productive.Typical Responsibilities IncludeManaging starters and leavers across Microsoft 365 and Active Directory.Installing, configuring and re-imaging laptops and devices.Enrolling new hardware and preparing devices for reuse.Handling support requests face-to-face, remotely and via ticketing system.Prioritising and progressing tickets, keeping colleagues updated.Maintaining accurate IT asset records.Coordinating IT equipment orders and liaising with suppliers.Supporting software licensing and basic contract administration.Assisting with upgrades and improvement work across the year.Creating and maintaining documentation and knowledge articles.Escalating to the MSP when needed, and seeing issues through to completion.Why this role is worth a lookReal ownership. You are the day-to-day IT point of contact, not one of many on a large helpdesk.Varied work. Users, devices, troubleshooting, onboarding, kit, suppliers, improvements.Support around you. Business Systems team and an MSP for escalation.Visible impact. Your work helps teams get their jobs done every day.Training support. Study support and development for role-relevant learning.Central Bristol. Easy commute and a stable, established employer.This is a great step up for someone who wants broader exposure and responsibility, without needing a “big corporate” IT structure around them.Salary & Benefits£30,000 salary.Up to 5% annual bonus (linked to personal objectives).25 days holiday + bank holidays (includes Christmas closure days).Option to buy or sell up to 5 days annual leave.1 day per week WFH after probation.Flexible hours (core hours 09:30–15:00).Early finish Fridays (14:00) if hours are made up during the week.Up to 5% employer pension contribution + NI rebate.Study support.Cycle to Work scheme.Cash plan, free online GP service, and wellbeing app discounts.Employee Assistance Programme (counselling + legal advice).Casual dress code and company social events.ApplyIf you want an IT Infrastructure Technician role where you can take ownership, support people directly, and keep building your Microsoft 365, Entra ID and Intune experience, apply now or contact us for a confidential conversation.LNKD1_UKTJ

title: Information Technology Support Technician
location: City Of Bristol, England, United Kingdom
description: We are looking for a proactive and hands-on IT Support Technician to join our busy IT team, supporting a group of schools across the organisation. This role involves a combination of remote support and on-site deskside assistance, as well as participation in project work, infrastructure maintenance, and the deployment and configuration of devices and systems.Reporting to the IT Manager, you will play an integral role within the IT Support Team, delivering a first-class service and ensuring that the needs of staff and students are met promptly and effectively.The ideal candidate will have previous experience in a similar IT support role and will be responsible for providing 1st and 2nd line support, administering IT systems, and following established support processes. There will also be opportunities to contribute to technical project delivery, with a strong focus on security, resilience, and proactive problem-solving.Key Responsibilities· Manage and act as the first point of contact for IT-related issues across all school sites via the service desk, phone, and in person.· Provide first line diagnosis, troubleshooting, and resolution for hardware, software, and network issues.· Escalate unresolved incidents to second line or external support partners where required.· Support the setup, maintenance, and deployment of laptops, desktops, iPads, Chromebooks, and other devices.· Assist with user account management in Microsoft 365, Azure AD, and Google Workspace.· Maintain accurate documentation, asset records, and service desk tickets.· Support the maintenance and monitoring of network infrastructure, printers, and classroom technology.· Ensure that IT security, safeguarding, and data protection procedures are followed at all times.· Travel to multiple school sites as required to deliver on-site support and scheduled maintenance.What we are looking forEssential experience and skills: Strong PC hardware/software troubleshooting skillsMinimum of 2 years’ experience in IT Support. Good standards of written and verbal English Ability to follow procedures/policies within an education setting Ability to work as part of a team and independentlyCan work under pressure and prioritise effectivelyFull Driving License and access to own vehicleWilling to travel nationallyDesirable experience and skills: Office 365 Administrative experienceKnowledge of firewalls, switches, and VPNsGoogle Classroom experienceKnowledge in Esport\Gaming System SetupsKnowledge in the Department For Education standards.Closing Date: 15th March 2026Salary: £28,000 - £30,000 + Car AllowanceContract: Full Time Start Date: ASAP

title: IT Support Technician
location: Kingston Upon Hull, England, United Kingdom
description: Location: HullSalary: £25,000 - £30,000Are you an IT Support technician who enjoys variety, ownership, and getting involved in projects not just tickets?Were partnering with a growing, multi-site business to recruit an IT Support administrator to support a close-knit IT technical team. description: Role: IT Hardware Technician (IMAC)Role type: Permanent, full timeLocation: Office based, Crawley (Three Bridges)Working hours: 9:00am - 5:30pm, Mon - FriSalary: up to £25,000 per annumAre you passionate about tech, hands-on work, and delivering great customer experiences? title: Senior Infrastructure Engineer
description: About AspireAt Aspire Technology Solutions, we look for dynamic individuals who want to work for one of the fastest growing IT and Cyber Security providers in the UK. It is an exciting time to join us on our journey as we grow and expand!Our mission: To deliver technology like no other!About The RoleWe are looking for a Senior Infrastructure Engineer to join our growing Infrastructure team based from our head office on the Gateshead Quayside.As a Senior Infrastructure Engineer, you will possess a strong technical background and extensive experience in technical support. Working alongside the wider Service Desk, you will work towards continually driving home technical solutions whilst putting our customer’s needs first.You will be an experienced engineer who is an instinctive problem solver and able to drive technical solutions. You will be responsible for investigating a wide range of technical issues and diagnosing and fixing critical IT issues. Strong communication skills will help you to diagnose the customer’s issue and explain technical issues in a way that non-experts can understand. Customer service is key to success in the role – living our values are woven through everything we do to guarantee that our service delivery is second to none. What you will be doingInvestigate and diagnose a wide range of escalated technical issues. Provide technical support to customers via email and telephone. Provide escalation support to infrastructure engineers. Identify recurring technical faults. Manage critical IT outages with customers to efficiently resolve the situation. Liaise with customers, stakeholders and team members to gather requirements and propose technical solutions. Ensure high levels of performance, availability and security of the infrastructure. Root cause analysis and future fault prevention. Continuously improve the infrastructure through automation and optimization. You will suit the role if you haveStrong technical background with a good understanding of MS Server, Virtualisation, Storage and Networking technologies. Proven experience in IT with a focus on technical support. Good communication and documentation skills. Ability to work well as part of a team or individually whilst building relationships with other departments. Willingness to make decisions, exhibit sound and accurate judgement and include the appropriate people in the decision-making process. Highly motivated and able to set and achieve challenging goals. Experience with Windows Server, Microsoft Exchange, Active Directory, Infrastructure Hardware and Storage, VMware, Microsoft Azure & 365 administration, and Citrix is preferred. Relevant certifications such as MCSE, VCP, AZ-104, AZ-800 are an advantage. Our valuesWe understand that you might not have all of the qualifications and experiences that we think are key to succeeding in this role, therefore we provide continuous learning and development at Aspire, which is at the heart of everything we do. As long as you are passionate, eager to learn and driven to be the best at what you do and live our values this role may be a fit for you.SalaryStarting salary, £30,000 - £44,000 per annum (depending on experience)BenefitsHere at Aspire we offer a great benefits package and a competitive salary. Being a people-centric organisation is what sets us apart – we strive to look after our people in the best way we can!Health Cash PlanCycle to Work SchemeNetwork BenefitsEmployee Assistance ProgramEnhanced Annual Leave Entitlement (increasing with length of service)Enhanced Maternity, Paternity and Parental SchemesEnhanced Pension SchemeTech Purchase SchemeElectric Vehicle Salary Sacrifice SchemeEmployee referral schemeNew business referral schemeDiscounted Gym MembershipLife Assurance and Critical Illness coverLocation and Working ArrangementsYou will be based at Aspire’s Gateshead office, with travel as required.Hours of WorkFull time (37.5 hours per week), Monday to Friday.Equality, Inclusion and Diversity at AspireAspire is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating a culture that respects and values each other’s differences, that promotes dignity, equality and diversity that encourages individuals to develop their true potential.Interview ProcessWe generally conduct interviews in three stages, lasting approximately 2-3 weeks depending on everyone’s calendars.Shortlisted candidates will be invited to a pre-screening call via teams to discuss your experience and what you’re looking for in your next role.Successful candidates will be invited to an interview with the Hiring Manager.If successful in interview, candidates will be invited to an informal interview with key stakeholders at Aspire to meet the wider team(s).#AspireForMore

title: IT Systems Engineer - 2nd Line Support
description: Desktop/Systems Engineer - Virtualisation - Azure - Networks - IT Support - 1st & 2nd Line progressing to 3rd Line - Good Benefits Package - £30,000-37,000 per annumWe're looking for a Systems Engineer with a good technical background and customer facing attitude to join a growing managed service provider (MSP). You will be working with their key customers to solve IT issues and escalate where necessary, but the expectation is that you are able to undertake tasks from 1st to 2nd or potentially 3rd line if possible.You will be working in a really supportive and collaborative team in a welcoming and open environment, with a clear path for growth and development. Its a great business with a brilliant leadership team too.You will technically have experience in:O365 Support and AdminAzure AdministrationFirewallsNetworking (VLAN, WAN)Virtualisation (Hyper-V, VMwareThere is some on-call support for P1 issues with a weekend rota 1 in 4, which you are financially compensated for and the Systems Engineers cover 7am-6pm so some slight variation in working patterns are needed.This is a great business who look after their people, please send a CV for more insights.

title: IT Support Engineer
description: Co-op Live continues to attract world-class talent and events, drawing comparisons to and surpassing the world’s greatest live entertainment venues in show programming, sound, fan experience, premium hospitality, food and beverage, and more. The UK’s largest live entertainment arena is driven by a dedicated team of music and live event lovers, passionate about offering every visitor, fan, artist, and partner an unrivalled experience, before, during and after every event, as well as giving back to our local community and our planet. Partnership is at our heart, with Co-op Live excelling through meaningful alliances with the likes of naming rights partner Co-op, united by a joint vision to be the very best. The first venue in the UK for owners and operators Oak View Group (OVG), Co-op Live spearheads expansion into Europe and rest of world, a jewel in the crown of a globally renowned portfolio. Role Purpose Love tech? Thrive under pressure? Enjoy being the person everyone turns to when something really needs fixing? This could be your stage.At Co-op Live, we’re looking for an IT Support Engineer to join our fast-paced, growing team delivering world-class live entertainment. You’ll be at the heart of the action, providing exceptional technical support and first-class customer service to keep our shows running smoothly.You’ll be the first point of contact for all things tech - triaging issues, resolving incidents, fulfilling service requests, managing user accounts, and making sure every interaction leaves our users confident and supported.ResponsibilitiesThis isn’t your typical 9–5 IT job.Our events are the main event - so flexibility is key. You’ll work across day and evening shifts, providing crucial event-day support to internal teams, clients, and third-party partners. When thousands of fans are heading through the doors, you’ll be behind the scenes making sure the tech performs just as well as the artists on stage.Network infrastructureAccess control systemsDigital signageCCTVServer environmentsWhen something needs attention, you’ll prioritise quickly, think on your feet, and keep everything running without missing a beat.This is a brilliant opportunity for someone ambitious, hands-on, and eager to grow. You’ll work alongside a passionate team, build your expertise in a unique live events environment, and play a vital role in delivering the biggest live entertainment experience in the UK.This is an onsite role, working 5 days out of 7 including weekends, because the show doesn’t stop, and neither do we.Knowledge, Skills And ExperienceWe would really like to hear from you, should you have the following experience –Worked as part of a service deskNetwork and firewall experienceRelevant IT Qualification / ExperienceExperience of management Windows server environmentsStrong communication skillsWhat Co-op Live Will Give To YouAlongside the benefit of working with an awesome team of talented people, we want to help you get the best out of your work and have a work life balance.Provide you with development opportunitiesKey objectives for you to succeed in your roleSalary: £30,000-£35,000 dependent on experience + Benefits25 days holiday plus bank holidaysEmployer pension schemePrivate Health Care scheme – VitalityLife Assurance 4 x basic salaryEyecare vouchersWeCare Employee Assistance Programme to support you and your family 24hrs, 7 days per week when you need the serviceCompany sick pay schemeEnhanced family friendly benefitsOffice refreshments and breakfast itemsTeam social eventsOur Inclusion CommitmentsOur venue is for the fans, the artists, everyone! Attracting a diverse workforce which reflects our local community is what will make our venue the best music venue in the UK!We understand that to continue positively disrupting the sports and live entertainment industry, we need a diverse team to help us do it. We also believe that inclusivity drives innovation, strengthens our people, improves our service, and raises our excellence. We are committed to creating a great place to work amplifying voices from all different backgrounds. There is something for everyone at Co-op Live, come and get involved.If you do require any additional support or reasonable adjustments to support your application, please let us know and we will work with you to ensure you have everything you need. We will consider applications from all candidates who are able to demonstrate the required skills necessary to fulfil the role. Employee Screening and Eligibility to Work We recognise the importance of safeguarding, dependent on role you may be required to undertake a Disclosure and Barring Service Check, details of which will be shared with you at interview.If successful in your application, you will be required to provide eligibility to work evidence in line with the ‘Eligibility to Work in the UK’ requirements.

title: Junior Network Engineer
description: Ashford, Kent (field-based with client travel)£30,000+ DOEAre you early in your networking career and keen to build real hands-on experience?Do you enjoy both technical configuration and physical infrastructure work?Have you worked in (or want exposure to) an MSP environment where no two days are the same?If so, this could be a strong next step.We are recruiting for a Junior Network Engineer to join a growing Managed Service Provider supporting a wide range of business clients.This is a genuinely varied, fast-paced role combining logical network engineering with structured cabling and physical installations. It will suit someone practical, proactive, and comfortable working across multiple client environments both remotely and on-site.You will gain exposure to enterprise-grade networking technologies while building solid real-world deployment experience.Due to the project-led nature of MSP work, the role involves regular client travel and occasional out-of-hours work.The RoleYou will support the title: IT Support Engineer
description: A well-established organisation in Glasgow is looking for an IT Support Engineer to join the IT team (hybrid).This is an exciting opportunity to provide high-quality first and second line support to internal users, helping to ensure the smooth operation of business-critical systems and a great end-user experience.What You’ll Be Doing:You’ll be at the heart of the organisation’s day-to-day IT operations, acting as a go-to expert for technical support across desktops, laptops, mobile devices. From onboarding new starters and provisioning equipment to diagnosing complex faults and resolving incidents, you’ll take real ownership of issues from start to finish. You’ll monitor and respond to alerts across modern security and cloud platforms such as Sophos, Zscaler, Azure and Microsoft 365, ensuring systems remain secure, stable and efficient. Working both on-site and remotely, you’ll collaborate with colleagues and third-party suppliers to troubleshoot issues, maintain hardware and software, and continuously improve IT services. This is a hands-on, varied role where no two days are the same so it’s ideal for someone who enjoys problem-solving, supporting people and making a visible impact.You’ll ideally have most of the following:You’ll be a confident and customer-focused IT support professional who thrives in a busy support environment. You’ll be comfortable managing your own workload while working collaboratively as part of a wider team, and you’ll deliver a professional service to users with varying levels of technical ability.** An IT-related qualification and/or relevant higher education** Hands-on technical support experience ** Experience supporting Microsoft-based environments** Experience working with / in an Azure environment ** Strong troubleshooting, communication and organisational skills.** Valid UK drivers’ licence and vehicle (occasional travel to sites) This role comes with a salary up to £35,000 + benefits and offers hybrid working in Glasgow (1/2 days in office). The office is a short walk from a train station and has onsite parking so is easy to get to. If this sounds interesting, please apply or contact Matt MacAlpine.

title: IT Support Field Engineer Within East Midlands
description: IT Support Field Engineer – East MidlandsHome based East Midlands| Hybrid workingPermanent | 37.5 hours per week (Mon–Fri, 8am–4pm)Salary: Up to £35,000 per annum (dependent on experience and qualifications) + £3,000 car allowance. In 2025, Outcomes First Group were officially certified as a ‘Great Place to Work’ for the sixth year running. We’re proud to be part of the 4-Day Working Week trial, giving our teams more time to recharge while still earning 100% of their pay.*Outcomes First Group is the largest independent provider of high-quality special needs education and care in the UK. We deliver research-led, personalised education that helps every child thrive.Our vision is to empower every child with a world-class education that nurtures potential and inspires lifelong learning. Our mission is to unlock that potential through personalised learning and opportunity. And our promise is simple: we listen, we work together, and we are accountable to one another to make the remarkable happen.The Role: We are seeking an experienced IT Support Field Engineer to provide 2nd/3rd line support across multiple sites in the East Midlands. You will assist with incidents, service requests, and problem resolution, escalating where necessary, and support the wider IT team with technology rollouts and investigations.What you'll do: Troubleshoot and resolve incidents, service requests, and major problems.Serve as first point of escalation for 1st/2nd line engineers and specific sites.Manage site relationships and support infrastructure, applications, devices, and networking equipment (Cisco Meraki, Unifi).Administer user accounts, permissions, and mobile device configurations (Active Directory, Office 365, Azure AD, Google Workspace, Jamf, Intune).Conduct root cause analysis, contribute to knowledge sharing, and support cyber and P1 infrastructure incidents.Install applications, configure hardware (laptops, mobiles, tablets), support printers, cloud telephony, servers, and web filtering.What we're looking for: 3–5 years’ experience in 2nd/3rd line IT support, preferably within ITIL frameworks or MSPs.Strong technical knowledge of desktop/server platforms, networking, virtualization, and cloud solutions.Excellent troubleshooting, communication, and organisational skills.Desirable: IT qualification, VOIP/telephony knowledge, and network management experience.Other Requirements:Travel to schools and office locations as required.Commitment to safeguarding children and vulnerable adults.Maintain professional development and training.BenefitsLife AssurancePension scheme with contribution options“Your Wellbeing Matters” mental and physical health supportFamily Growth Support, including enhanced maternity and paternity leaveFlexible benefits via Vista (discounts, Cycle to Work, Electric Car Scheme, critical illness cover and more)We are committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and young people. title: Infrastructure Support Engineer
description: Infrastructure Support Engineer – Technology Services💰 £35,000 per year 💼 Full-time, Permanent 📍 Poole (Hybrid working available)Our client is a well-established technology services provider with nearly 30 years’ experience helping businesses perform better through the power of technology. Operating with a strong people-first culture and a commitment to exceptional customer experience, they work as a trusted IT partner to their clients rather than just a support provider.They are now looking for an Infrastructure Support Engineer to join their growing Service Support team in a customer-facing role focused on delivering high-quality, reliable, and empathetic technical support.You’ll work across Microsoft 365, server infrastructure, and networking technologies, helping customers operate securely, efficiently, and confidently. This role is ideal for someone with 2+ years’ experience in an MSP or IT reseller environment who enjoys problem-solving, customer interaction, and continuous learning.You’ll collaborate closely with customers, internal engineers, and service teams to deliver high-quality support while contributing to internal projects and continuous service improvement.The RoleAs an Infrastructure Support Engineer, you will deliver exceptional 1st and 2nd line support via phone, email, and messaging, act as a trusted point of contact for customers by owning and resolving technical issues, and support Microsoft 365 environments including Exchange Online and Entra ID.You’ll administer and troubleshoot Windows Server and Active Directory environments, investigate and resolve infrastructure, desktop, and networking issues, and document all work clearly in line with defined support frameworks. You’ll also collaborate with internal teams on projects and service improvements, occasionally provide onsite customer support, and proactively identify opportunities to improve customer environments.Key ResponsibilitiesMicrosoft 365 administration and support (must-have)Desktop and end-user support across Microsoft applicationsWindows Server administration including DNS, DHCP, and Group PolicyAzure, Hyper-V, and VMware troubleshootingNetworking fundamentals (WAN, LAN, WLAN, VLAN – desirable)Backup, security, and endpoint protection supportClear communication with technical and non-technical usersMaintaining high service and documentation standardsWhat We’re Looking ForRequired skills and experience include 2+ years’ experience in an infrastructure or support engineering role, ideally within an MSP or IT reseller environment, strong Microsoft 365 and Active Directory knowledge, solid Windows Server and desktop support experience, and an understanding of core networking principles.You’ll be a confident and professional communicator with strong troubleshooting and organisational skills, and you’ll be willing to attend customer sites when required.Desirable experience includes exposure to Intune, Microsoft Teams VoIP, networking devices, Azure infrastructure, and security best practices.Why Apply?Salary of £35,000 per yearFull-time, permanent positionHybrid working model25 days holiday plus bank holidaysHealthcare cash plan and employee assistance programmeBirthday off 🎉Strong focus on training, development, and career progressionSupportive, people-first culture with long-term career opportunitiesIf you’re an Infrastructure Support Engineer who enjoys customer interaction, technical ownership, and making a real difference to end users, this could be a great next step.📩 Apply now or reach out to Emma Pretorius to start the conversation.

ITIL Framework Understanding Familiarity with the ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) framework, demonstrating an understanding of best practices in IT service management  Time Management: Ability to work to a deadline, cope with the pressure of multi-tasking and able to prioritise work.  Exceptional Customer Service Skills:  Outstanding interpersonal skills and a customer-centric approach, ensuring a positive and professional experience for end-users. Additionally, knowledge of remote desktop tools, familiarity with the ITIL framework, and exceptional customer service skills are essential for success in this position  About UsSCC is Europe's largest privately-owned IT business, based out of the new £7m HQ office in Birmingham and we help clients succeed through IT transformation and exceptional customer experiences. title: TechOps Specialist / IT Support Specialist
description: About UsPerk (formerly TravelPerk) is the intelligent platform for travel and spend management. Built to tackle the time-consuming, manual work that gets in the way of real work, our tools automate everything from travel bookings to expenses, invoice processing, and more. By eliminating this shadow work that wastes hours, erodes morale, and saps innovation, we’re on a mission to power real work, with real impact.We’re trusted by more than 10,000 companies worldwide, including Wise, On Running, Breitling, and Fabletics, and we’re tackling the 7 hours of lost productivity per employee each week, a $1.7 trillion problem.Founded in 2015, Perk has grown into a global company of more than 1,800 people across 12 offices globally, with headquarters in London and Boston. We combine innovation, control, and simplicity to transform how businesses work and how people feel at work.At Perk, we’re driven by our values, like being an owner, delivering a 7-star experience, and working as one team. We value curiosity, purpose, and mindset, not just knowledge, to unlock the power in your potential. Our talent team brings together leading minds from the travel and SaaS industries, representing over 70 countries. If you’re excited about having a real impact and shaping how millions of people experience work, we’d love you on the team.Visit www.perk.com to learn more.Location: Birmingham (On-site, within commuting distance to the hub)About the Role:We are looking for an ambitious, motivated and knowledgeable TechOps Specialist to help build a world-class IT experience at Perk.In this role you will be an instrumental part of every aspect of IT at Perk. You will be responsible for everything from helping users day-to-day to delivering big projects that alter the course of the company.You will need to be smart, adaptable, comfortable with ambiguity and people-focused. You should have a background in something technical and love learning how things work in IT. The more experience the better!This role needs to be based in Birmingham and requires you to be in the office at least 4 days per week.What will my day look like?As a TechOps Specialist you would…Be part of a team global TechOps team, supporting our 1,200 Perk employees across all of our lPerk hubs (London, Edinburgh, Barcelona, Berlin, Birmingham, Chicago, Miami, Boston)Working on tickets in our ticketing system, providing route analysis along with improving our processes; ZendeskAssist with the the lifecycle of lPerk assets (catalog, repair, ship, inventory, and purchasing)Configuring/maintaining our MDM tools, for example deploying new configurations, application managementDeploying software to our endpoint devices (Windows/macOS)Administration of Okta, creating workflows, implementing new/existing SaaS using SAML, SCIM, leveraging API’s, directory managementManaging Google Workspace, managing user/groups, mailboxes/email,, mailflows, policies based on OU, GoogleAppsHelp to maintain our network infrastructure and associated systems through core skills and change managementSecurely configure and maintain our suite of SaaS toolsProviding audio visual support (Meeting Rooms, Company Events) using Zoom/Zoom RoomsMentoring junior members of the team, effective documentation, show and tells, IT shadowing and general knowledge sharingAssisting/managing projects within your domainLiaising with third party vendors when requiredOccasional out of hours supportWhat skills and experience are required?Experienced in a face paced environment, ability to adapt accordinglyExperienced providing internal IT Support, providing a 7 star experienceOperational experience supporting Windows, macOS, mobile devicesAdministration experience implementing & managing modern MDM tools; Microsoft Intune and/or Jamf ProAdministration experience managing identity management tools; Okta & Active DirectoryAdministration experience managing Google WorkspaceStrong documentation skillsExperience managing modern SAAS tools (Slack, Notion, Box & Zoom)Experience supporting network infrastructure; DHCP, LAN, WAN, DNS, WIFAutomation/Scripting experienceHow We WorkAt Perk, we take an IRL-first approach to work, where our team works together in-person 3 days a week. As such, this role requires you to be based within commuting distance of our hubs. We fundamentally believe in the value of meeting in real life to improve connectivity, productivity, creativity and ultimately making us a great place to work.For certain roles, we can help with relocation from anywhere in the world, English is the official language at the office. Please submit your resume in English if you choose to apply. Do not forget to submit an updated portfolio and/or resume.Perk is a global company with a diverse customer base, and we want to make sure the people behind our product reflect that. We’re an equal opportunity employer, which means you’re welcome at Perk regardless of how you look, where you’re from, or anything else that makes you, well, you.Protect Yourself from Recruitment ScamsAll official communication from Perk will always come from email addresses ending in @perk.com or @externalperk.com, our verified social media channels, or recruiters listed on our official LinkedIn page. We will never ask you to pay for equipment, training, or fees, request sensitive personal information such as bank details early in the process, or communicate through unofficial apps like WhatsApp, Telegram, or Signal. If you receive a message claiming to be from Perk that seems suspicious, please do not respond. Forward it to security [at] perk .com, and we will confirm whether it is legitimate.Compensation Range: $75K - $100K
